question: Which advice is most appropriate for toddler health concerns?

options.text: Ignore mild symptoms unless fever is present
options.explanation: Toddlers can have serious illness without fever, so symptoms should not be ignored.

options.text: Wait several days before calling the provider
options.explanation: Delaying care can miss important warning signs and is not recommended.

options.text: Trust your intuition and call your health care provider if worried
options.explanation: Parental concern matters, and the guidance says to trust your intuition and call the provider right away if worried.

options.text: Avoid well-child visits unless the child is ill
options.explanation: Well-child visits are important for immunizations, screening, and monitoring growth and development.

question: What is the main purpose of routine well-baby checkups?

options.text: To monitor growth and development and catch problems early
options.explanation: Well-baby exams help ensure normal growth and development and allow early detection of problems.

options.text: To treat all illnesses before symptoms appear
options.explanation: Checkups do not treat every illness before it appears; they focus on monitoring and prevention.

options.text: To replace emergency care for infants
options.explanation: Routine checkups do not replace emergency care when a baby is acutely ill.

options.text: To avoid the need for vaccinations
options.explanation: Well-baby visits include needed immunizations rather than avoiding them.
